claim: Current implementation and tests support a demand evidence flow where brownfield or technical-risk demands require explorer, cross-checker, and verifier evidence before PRD readiness; dispatch defaults to dry-run, requires explicit execution authorization, feeds agent results back into PRD readiness, and blocks readiness on missing agent results or evidence conflicts. Read-only enforcement is explicit in agent prompts and Codex/custom dispatch config, but Claude provider invocation defaults to its configured permission mode unless set separately.

evidence.summary: Defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier evidence agent protocols; each has writes_code false and uses yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1.
evidence.why: Establishes the structured, read-only evidence roles required by the demand workflow.

evidence.summary: Technical signals such as field, schema, api, auth, data flow, migration, database, and state drive careful routing and cross_check evidence policy.
evidence.why: Supports the claim that brownfield/API/schema-risk demand facts trigger careful/cross-check handling before PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: PRD readiness checks required slots, active blockers, open questions, unresolved assumptions, required evidence roles, and evidence agreement; prd_ready is true only when blockers are empty.
evidence.why: Shows missing evidence, assumptions, blockers, or conflicts prevent PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: For each required evidence role, readiness adds EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED when no completed result with evidence and payload is present.
evidence.why: Directly supports the current blocked status requiring explorer, cross-checker, and verifier results.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement groups results by claim and emits EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT when recommendations include block or mixed proceed/clarify/cross_check recommendations.
evidence.why: Shows agent disagreement remains a blocker rather than being averaged away.

evidence.summary: buildDemandSessionState computes triage, readiness, evidence tasks, needed evidence agents, stage, blockers, and next action; blocked status maps to DEMAND_NOT_PRD_READY.
evidence.why: Connects router readiness to session state and the evidence stage used by the current demand status.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan creates actions from status.state.evidence_tasks, sets default_mode dry_run, requires executeAgents=true and allowAgentDispatch=true, marks writes_business_code false, and labels agent_instruction read_only.
evidence.why: Shows dispatch planning is explicit, dry-run by default, and declares read-only evidence-agent execution policy.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt embeds hard rules requiring READ ONLY behavior, no assumptions as facts, missing/assumptions separation, and one JSON object output in the required evidence result shape.
evidence.why: Shows provider agents are instructed to return structured JSON evidence and avoid edits.

evidence.summary: executionConfig sets codex_sandbox to read-only and codex_approval to never for Codex, and sets custom_sandbox to read-only for custom providers.
evidence.why: Supports read-only enforcement for Codex/custom provider configurations during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: Codex provider invocation passes --sandbox from ai.codex_sandbox and approval_policy from ai.codex_approval.
evidence.why: Confirms the read-only Codex config from evidence dispatch is used in the actual provider command.

evidence.summary: Claude provider invocation passes --permission-mode from ai.claude_permission_mode or defaults to default.
evidence.why: Limits the read-only claim: evidence dispatch does not set claude_permission_mode in the inspected code, so Claude read-only behavior is not proven by dispatch config alone.

evidence.summary: Runtime starts in dry_run unless execute is requested; if execute is requested without allowAgentDispatch, status stays blocked with DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_DISPATCH_NOT_ALLOWED and no agent execution proceeds.
evidence.why: Shows dispatch cannot run provider agents without explicit authorization.

evidence.summary: When authorized, dispatch spawns each provider prompt, parses JSON, normalizes agent results, optionally writes evidence artifacts, then calls inspectDemandPrdReadiness with evidence_results and returns pass or blocked based on readiness.
evidence.why: Shows explorer/cross-checker/verifier outputs are fed back into readiness and can keep PRD blocked.

evidence.summary: Test asserts an existing inventory API schema field change routes to careful, cross_check, and requires explorer, cross-checker, and verifier.
evidence.why: Validates the router behavior for brownfield technical contract changes.

evidence.summary: Test asserts cross-check PRD readiness is false with only explorer evidence and true only after explorer, cross-checker, and verifier completed results are present.
evidence.why: Validates required evidence-agent completion gating PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Test asserts existing blockers and evidence-agent conflicts prevent PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Validates conflict/blocker preservation in readiness.

evidence.summary: Test asserts dispatch plan actions are explorer, cross-checker, verifier and execution policy is dry_run with writes_business_code false.
evidence.why: Validates planned evidence dispatch shape and safety policy.

evidence.summary: Tests assert dry-run does not spawn provider agents and execute mode without allowAgentDispatch blocks with no agent_results.
evidence.why: Validates explicit authorization requirement.

evidence.summary: Test asserts authorized dispatch calls explorer, cross-checker, verifier in order, returns pass readiness, and records all three agent results.
evidence.why: Validates dispatch-to-readiness integration when all evidence agents pass.

evidence.summary: Test asserts a cross-checker block recommendation produces blocked dispatch status, prd_ready false, and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blocker.
evidence.why: Validates conflict remains a blocker after dispatch.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states PRD readiness requires all slots and no blockers/unconfirmed assumptions/missing evidence; cross_check requires cross-checker; dispatch defaults to dry-run, requires both execute flags, and conflicts keep readiness blocked.
evidence.why: Provides documented contract matching the implementation and tests.

evidence.summary: Workflow command description says demand dispatch is explicit, defaults to dry-run, requires explicit execute and allow flags, and evidence agents must not edit source files.
evidence.why: Shows the public workflow contract for demand dispatch safety and usage.

risks: If Claude is used without claude_permission_mode=read-only, the inspected dispatch code proves a read-only prompt but not provider-level read-only enforcement for Claude.
If dispatch is run with writeArtifact enabled, it writes evidence artifacts under the output directory; this does not modify source code but is still a filesystem write.
If current tests have regressed, the source-level evidence remains valid but test-pass confidence is unverified in this read-only exploration.
missing: Independent cross-checker and verifier evidence results are still required by the current demand protocol before PRD readiness can be treated as complete.
Current test execution status was not verified in this exploration to avoid side effects; no claim is made that the test suite currently passes.
Need confirmation or code change if the requirement means all providers, including Claude, must be provider-enforced read-only rather than prompt-instructed read-only.

result.notes: Primary implementation, tests, and doctrine support the evidence dispatch/readiness blocker behavior. The only weakened claim is provider-level read-only enforcement for Claude, which is not proven by the inspected dispatch config.
